issues.title: jsonata transform fails with PayloadTooLargeError
issues.body:
The JSONata transform pod fails for large payloads:

```
Using console tracing exporter
Jsonata HTTP server listening on port 8080
PayloadTooLargeError: request entity too large
    at readStream (/app/node_modules/raw-body/index.js:163:17)
    at getRawBody (/app/node_modules/raw-body/index.js:116:12)
    at read (/app/node_modules/body-parser/lib/read.js:79:3)
    at jsonParser (/app/node_modules/body-parser/lib/types/json.js:138:5)
    at Layer.handle [as handle_request] (/app/node_modules/express/lib/router/layer.js:95:5)
    at trim_prefix (/app/node_modules/express/lib/router/index.js:328:13)
    at /app/node_modules/express/lib/router/index.js:286:9
    at Function.process_params (/app/node_modules/express/lib/router/index.js:346:12)
    at next (/app/node_modules/express/lib/router/index.js:280:10)
    at /app/jsonata.js:187:9
```

I suspect it is using the default express body limit of 100kb:

https://github.com/knative-extensions/eventing-integrations/blob/5dc868ad46bf6e246ca74192beb710ca3a54bafb/transform-jsonata/jsonata.js#L190

This is quite a bit lower than what other sinks supports, e.g. AWS SNS has a limit of 256kb.

Would it be possible to make this limit much larger, or at least configurable?

issues.title: [FR][AWS] Support for AWS IRSA
issues.body:
Currently, only static credentials are supported. In EKS, it exists a feature called [IRSA](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/eks/latest/userguide/iam-roles-for-service-accounts.html) (IAM roles for service accounts), whihc will use k8s OIDC to assign credential to a pod. To use it, you simply need to use a service account with a specific annotation. `ContainerSource` already support passing a custom service account, however, the credentials are expected to be set via `CAMEL_KAMELET_AWS_..._ACCESSKEY` and `CAMEL_KAMELET_AWS_..._SECRETKEY`.

With IRSA, the pod-identity controller will mutate the pod to inject the AWS credentials, like so:
```
    env:                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   
    - name: AWS_STS_REGIONAL_ENDPOINTS                                                                                                                                                                                                     
      value: regional                                                                                                                                                                                                                      
    - name: AWS_DEFAULT_REGION                                                                                                                                                                                                             
      value: eu-west-1                                                                                                                                                                                                                     
    - name: AWS_REGION                                                                                                                                                                                                                     
      value: eu-west-1                                                                                                                                                                                                            
    - name: AWS_ROLE_ARN                                                                                                                                                                                                                   
      value: arn:aws:iam::<AccountNumber>:role/my-role                                                                                                                                                    
    - name: AWS_WEB_IDENTITY_TOKEN_FILE                                                                                                                                                                                                    
      value: /var/run/secrets/eks.amazonaws.com/serviceaccount/token   
```

AWS client will automatically use these, in absence of static credential.

Relates to https://github.com/knative/eventing/issues/8437 and potentially https://github.com/knative/eventing/issues/8544

issues.title: [AWS S3 Source] Unuspported ignoreBody option
issues.body:
The `ignoreBody` doesn't seem to have any effect. Here is a minimal reproduction steps:

```yaml
apiVersion: sources.knative.dev/v1alpha1
kind: IntegrationSource
metadata:
  name: integration-source-aws-s3
spec:
  aws:
    s3:
      arn: "my-bucket"
      ignoreBody: true
      region: eu-west-1
    auth:
      secret:
        ref:
          name: "aws-s3-secret"
  sink:
    ref:
      apiVersion: eventing.knative.dev/v1
      kind: Broker
      name: default
```

## Expect

Events without the S3 object content as message

## Actuall

Same than without the option, the S3 object content is used as message

version: latest

issues.title: `TestIntegrationSourceWithTLS` fail when OIDC is enabled, but sink has no audience set
issues.body:
When running the `TestIntegrationSourceWithTLS` e2e test in an environment with OIDC being enabled, the test fails due to

```
org.apache.camel.CamelException: HTTP operation failed invoking https://sink-ffkrugnv.test-xzkifhsn.svc.cluster.local/ caused by: java.io.FileNotFoundException: /oidc/token does not exist, /oidc/token does not exist
```
in the integration source,

This is, because the sinkbinding/containersource reconciler which creates the token secret, create the secret only, when OIDC is enabled AND the sink has an audience. As the test does not define an audience for the sink (and not all sinks have an audience (e.g. custom ones)), this token is not created always and thus the volume not mounted.

This should be fixed, so the IntegrationSource only uses the OIDC token, when required.

pullRequests.title: Configure multi-architecture support for JIB in eventing-integrations
pullRequests.body:
## fix: [#8721](https://github.com/knative/eventing/issues/8721)
Set up multi-architecture JIB configuration in eventing-integrations POM.
Added amd64, arm64, s390x, and ppc64le platforms to allow building container images for multiple CPU architectures.  
This matches the approach used in eventing-kafka-broker and ensures proper image support for KinD e2e tests.

pullRequests.title: Fix Transform-jsonata image path for eventing-transformations-images.yaml
pullRequests.body:
Currently we have invalid paths to the jsonata image in the eventing-transformations-images.yaml:

```
apiVersion: v1
kind: ConfigMap
metadata:
  name: eventing-transformations-images
  namespace: knative-eventing
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/version: "v1.19.0"
data:
  transform-jsonata: gcr.io/knative-releases/transform-jsonata:v1.19.0@sha256:d362cb7ad8b779a83d66d136e5de0d51bd764698d216df9f3ead1eb76bfd7d70
```

This PR addresses it and fixes the path of the transform-jsonata image

pullRequests.title: Add Jsonata transformation server
pullRequests.body:
The transformation allows users to modify event payloads using JSONata expressions, making it easier to extract, manipulate, and structure event data before forwarding it to subscribers.

This transformation simplifies event processing by allowing declarative message modifications, reducing the need for additional middleware or custom processing services.

issueComments.body:
yes, in addition to the eventBus setting with `CAMEL_KAMELET_AWS_EVENTBRIDGE_SINK_EVENTBUS_NAME_OR_ARN` you need to provide some more mandatory user input in the form of CloudEvent attributes when sending events to the sink.

In particular this is a set of CloudEvents extension attributes that need to be set in the event to the sink:

- `ce-resources-arn` to specify the ARN of resources related to the AWS event (e.g. `arn:aws:s3:eu-east-1:000000000001:test`)
- `ce-event-source` to specify the event source related to the AWS event (e.g. `aws.s3`)
- `ce-detail-type` to specify the detail type related to the AWS event (e.g. `Object Created`)

See [#mandatory-user-input](https://github.com/knative-extensions/eventing-integrations/tree/main/aws-eventbridge-sink#mandatory-user-input)

Could we do some auto mapping? 

e.g. that we can just simply forward CEs to that sink?

```yaml
apiVersion: sinks.knative.dev/v1alpha1
kind: IntegrationSink
metadata:
  name: eventbridge-sink
  namespace: knative-samples
spec:
  aws:
    eventbridge:
      arn: "arn:aws:events:us-east-1:124345678:event-bus/my-event-bus"
      region: "us-east-1"
    auth:
      secret:
        ref:
          name: "my-secret"
```
So you would need some envVar settings instead of using the CouldEvent attributes right?

Can't we map the CE-source directly to the ce-event-source on the data plane? 

For the detail, I guess I could have some field on the `eventbridge` property
I have added the opportunity to set default values via EnvVar settings. @matzew please have a look: https://github.com/knative-extensions/eventing-integrations/pull/100
